concerns.text: The plan explicitly states it will 'generate synthetic image patches' and 'simulate BGC-Argo-like data' because the raw MODIS rasters and in-situ data are not verified. This dataset choice is inappropriate for the research question 'How do oceanographic conditions drive... phytoplankton distribution?' which requires empirical observation of real-world correlations. Synthetic data cannot validate the relationship between real environmental drivers and biological abundance; it only tests the model architecture's ability to fit noise or pre-defined distributions. The plan must identify a real, accessible proxy dataset (e.g., a smaller, verified subset of SeaBASS or a specific regional MODIS product) rather than simulating the core scientific signal.

The 'Verified datasets' block lists a MODIS metadata CSV but admits the full raster data is not verified, leading to the decision to use synthetic data. This renders the VLM training (FR-003) and the comparison to the Random Forest baseline (FR-002) scientifically invalid for the stated goal. A VLM trained on synthetic ocean color patches and synthetic text prompts cannot demonstrate that multi-modal architectures offer value in oceanography. The plan must cite a real, accessible dataset that provides both the image (ocean color) and the corresponding environmental metadata to satisfy the 'Multi-Modal Data Independence' principle without fabrication.
